retained_kernel_summary.producer: query-major rocWMMA K16xD16 contribution tile over H64
retained_kernel_summary.accumulator: LDS open-addressed hash table keyed by compressed group
retained_kernel_summary.slot_resolution: one hash lookup per selected K row, then D16 local atomicAdd into the resolved slot
retained_kernel_summary.global_emit: one global atomicAdd row per occupied hash slot and D16 block after local accumulation
retained_kernel_summary.diagnostic_contract: consumes materialized probs and grad_logits_scaled; does not yet fuse probability, LI, or compressor recompute

rejected_branches.read: Over-conservative hash table increased slot init/emit cost and did not approach the sparse atomic gate.
Widening selected tiles did not reduce runtime, so CTA count was not the primary wall.

rejected_branches.result: S128 parity failed before a fixed emit-index bug; the safer sentinel variant then developed a spin/performance cliff at S512
rejected_branches.read: Active slot emission alone is not enough, and sentinel reservation adds too much synchronization risk for this path.

diagnosis.root_cause: Moving the many-to-one dKV accumulation from global atomics into LDS atomics does not solve the S4096 wall; the local atomic/add and hash-table management become slower than the current sparse global atomic path.
diagnosis.what_changed: The rewrite reduced global writes but introduced per-selected-row hash lookup plus D16 local atomics and table initialization/scanning. That is acceptable at S128/S512 but explodes at S4096 layer-2 actual LI fan-in.
diagnosis.next_kernel_requirement: Do not use local atomics as the main coalescing mechanism. The next rewrite needs warp/block segmented reduction or group-owned MFMA accumulation that produces one coalesced group row without per-contribution atomicAdd, ideally fused with probability/LI/compressor recompute so selected partials are not materialized.
